sources.name: tempdbFileConfiguration
sources.purpose: Resolve whether a percent-only limit has a finite effective TempDB maximum.
sources.readCondition: At least one visible workload group has a percent limit and no MB limit.

sources.name: sessionUsageComparison
sources.purpose: Keep session-scoped allocation counters separate from workload-group current and peak counters.

effectiveLimitRules: A configured MB limit takes precedence over a configured percent limit.
A percent-only limit is effective only when the visible TempDB data-file configuration yields a supported finite maximum.
Stored limits are not reported as effective while Resource Governor is disabled or reconfiguration is pending.
Failure to read TempDB file configuration limits only percent-only workload groups; fixed MB and no-limit rows remain evaluable.
No configured limit is a valid neutral state and not a finding.
Current use, peak use, violations, and statistics start time remain runtime evidence even when no limit is configured.
falsePositiveBoundaries: No configured TempDB workload-group limit is not an error.
A violation count proves that enforcement occurred in the statistics window; it does not identify the responsible statement or root cause.
Workload-group counters are not additive with session allocation counters and may use different scopes.
Version store and the TempDB transaction log are outside this Resource Governor limit.
A stored percent limit can be ineffective because of the TempDB data-file configuration.
A configured value can differ from the active value while Resource Governor reconfiguration is pending.
Missing access to TempDB file configuration limits percent-only effectiveness evidence and does not invalidate fixed MB limits.

privacy.runtimeFixtures: Synthetic Example* workload groups and users only
